channel.title: quant-ph (TEST FEED)
channel.description: Local test feed for the notifier. Not real-time data.

item.title: Geometric Algebra Quantum Gate Decomposition
item.description: arXiv:2606.99001 Announce Type: new Abstract: Quantum gates are usually described through matrix and tensor-product formalisms that often obscure their geometric structure. In this work, we formulate the Pauli and Clifford groups within the complex Geometric Algebra (GA) framework. We further prove that Clifford operators are generated by products of pi/4-Pauli rotors and introduce a greedy Pauli rotor decomposition algorithm whose empirical behavior suggests unexpectedly compact decompositions for Clifford operators. Finally, we show that Clifford+T universality admits a natural geometric interpretation through pi/8-rotors within this framework.
item.dc:creator: Youssef Amraoui, Zeno Toffano

item.title: A high-threshold decoder for surface codes under biased noise
item.description: arXiv:2606.99002 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present a fault-tolerant decoding scheme for the surface code tailored to biased Pauli noise. Our decoder improves the code distance threshold and reduces the logical error rate compared with standard minimum-weight perfect matching, demonstrating improved performance for practical stabilizer codes.
item.dc:creator: Alice Researcher, Bob Collaborator

item.title: Entanglement distribution rates over lossy quantum repeater networks
item.description: arXiv:2606.99003 Announce Type: new Abstract: We analyze entanglement distribution across a chain of quantum repeaters in a quantum internet setting. Using entanglement swapping and purification at memory nodes, we derive achievable rates for the quantum network and compare link-layer scheduling protocols.
item.dc:creator: Carol Quantum

item.title: Device-independent quantum key distribution with improved noise tolerance
item.description: arXiv:2606.99004 Announce Type: new Abstract: We propose a device-independent QKD protocol that tolerates higher noise via randomness expansion. We prove security against general attacks and discuss the relevance to post-quantum cryptographic infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Dan Secure, Eve Adversary

item.title: Strongly correlated electrons in twisted bilayer systems
item.description: arXiv:2606.99005 Announce Type: cross Abstract: This condensed-matter paper studies strongly correlated electrons and superconductivity in twisted bilayer materials. It is cross-listed to quant-ph only tangentially and should be filtered out by the notifier.
item.dc:creator: Frank Solid

item.title: Benchmarking two-qubit gate fidelity in superconducting transmon processors
item.description: arXiv:2606.99006 Announce Type: new Abstract: We report calibration and crosstalk mitigation techniques that improve two-qubit gate fidelity in a superconducting transmon quantum processor, providing an effective pathway toward fault-tolerant photonic and solid-state quantum computing hardware.
item.dc:creator: Grace Hardware
